name: ai-assistant-button
title: AI Assistant Button
description: Inline or floating assistant trigger with unread-count, attention, and open states.

props.name: children
props.description: Visible label. Omit for an icon-only trigger, then set `label`.

props.name: unread
props.description: New assistant output waiting. `true` renders a dot; a number renders a count, capped at 9+.

props.name: attention
props.description: The assistant needs the user back — e.g. a queued draft finished. Renders a pulsing warning dot.

props.name: open
props.description: Whether the assistant surface is open. Swaps the icon and sets aria-expanded.

props.name: placement
props.description: Floating placements are fixed-position and carry the primary glow.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible name for the icon-only form; also the default tooltip.

clinicalNotes: The badge means "new assistant output", never "urgent clinical event". Do not reuse it for a critical result, a deteriorating patient, or anything with a time-to-treat — those need an interruptive surface that names the reason and offers an escalation path (see ai-escalation-alert), not a dot on a floating button a clinician may never open. Reserve `attention` for assistant-workflow completion, e.g. a draft is ready for review. Keep counts honest: a badge that is always lit is ignored within one shift.
notes: The unread/attention state is mirrored in a visually hidden string so it is never colour-only. Icon-only form takes its accessible name from `label`.
